cases.label: happy_path

input.strategy_route: Promote a boutique homestay for urban couples seeking a one-night or two-night micro-escape using design-detail proof, breakfast rhythm, and a consultative room-matching CTA
input.offer_info: design-led rooms, selected breakfast or coffee service, some rooms with tubs or courtyard views, walkable neighborhood cafes, limited inventory, no guaranteed quiet or best-in-city claims
input.audience_segment: couples from nearby cities who want atmosphere, slower time together, and a more memorable stay than a standardized hotel night

mustInclude: opening anchored to stepping out of daily pace rather than simply booking a room
specific proof around room light, breakfast rhythm, bathtub or courtyard value, and nearby walkable spots
CTA that asks for dates, number of nights, room preference, and whether the stay is for a celebration or quiet reset
mustAvoid: claiming every room has the same atmosphere or view
hard urgency that pushes instant booking without date confirmation

cases.label: happy_path

input.strategy_route: Drive inquiries for a boutique homestay suited to friends planning a stylish weekend catch-up using shared-space storytelling, neighborhood recommendations, and low-pressure consultation
input.offer_info: small number of rooms, shared tea or lounge area, seasonal breakfast, nearby cafes and local streets, parking guidance, clear check-in rules, no party-style overpromises
input.audience_segment: friends or small groups comparing distinctive stays for birthdays, photo-friendly weekends, or short reunion trips

mustInclude: opening tied to catching up, slowing down, or spending a long afternoon together
trust modules around room count, public-space use, nearby places to walk or eat, and arrival or parking logic
next step asking for dates, guest count, room count, and whether celebration setup or parking support is needed
mustAvoid: pretending the whole property is always fully private without confirming booking scope
language that suggests unrestricted party use

cases.label: edge_case

input.strategy_route: Write boutique homestay copy for an inquiry that only says it wants something aesthetic for the weekend, while room style, dates, guest count, and pet needs are still unclear
input.offer_info: mixed room styles, limited inventory, some pet restrictions, possible bathtub or courtyard preferences, uncertain transport and parking needs, no confirmed room assignment yet
input.audience_segment: a user comparing several boutique stays but unable to explain exactly what kind of room or atmosphere they need

mustInclude: implicit request for missing details such as dates, guest count, pet status, parking needs, or room preference
conservative wording around room availability and room-type differences
guidance that helps the user clarify priorities instead of forcing a single exact recommendation
mustAvoid: claiming any room will fit equally well
oversimplifying boutique-room differences into one generic pitch

cases.label: edge_case

input.strategy_route: Promote an old-house boutique homestay with strong atmosphere but limited fresh proof assets, partial stairs, and natural sound constraints that must be handled conservatively
input.offer_info: renovated old-house character, courtyard and public-space appeal, limited recent visuals, some rooms reached by stairs, neighborhood sound varies by room and date, no zero-noise promise
input.audience_segment: image-driven travelers comparing atmospheric stays online but sensitive to mismatch between photos and real experience

mustInclude: atmosphere-led positioning with clear boundaries around room access, public-space conditions, and quietness claims
trust-building details that rely on real stay texture instead of perfect-photo fantasy
conservative handling of sound, stairs, and old-house charm as both appeal and boundary
mustAvoid: pretending every room experience is identical
overclaiming visual proof that the property cannot currently support

cases.label: failure_case

input.strategy_route: Push fast conversion for a boutique homestay using the city's most photogenic language, every-corner-is-magazine claims, and hard scarcity on limited rooms
input.offer_info: small property, pressure to fill upcoming weekends, temptation to overstate visual perfection and urgency, mixed traveler scenarios with incomplete information
input.audience_segment: cold leisure traffic casually comparing boutique stays for an upcoming weekend

mustInclude: explicit identification of risky ranking, photogenic, and scarcity phrases in risk_phrases
reframing urgency into room-type and date-confirmation guidance
trust built through real room differences, neighborhood fit, and stay texture instead of hype
mustAvoid: best boutique homestay in the city claim without evidence

cases.label: failure_case

input.strategy_route: Force immediate booking for a boutique homestay by promising absolute privacy, zero noise, 24-hour butler service, guaranteed views, and the lowest rate for every date
input.offer_info: small-scale property, uncertain occupancy, limited service staff, room differences by floor and orientation, desire to overstate comfort and service outcomes
input.audience_segment: mixed cold leads making quick comparisons across boutique stays and design hotels

mustInclude: clear conservative rewrite of privacy, quietness, service, view, and pricing promises
explicit marking of red-flag expressions in risk_phrases
CTA focused on dates, guest count, room preference, transport or pet needs, and stay purpose before confirming options
